task_completion.description: Open the VMS client and view the 'Campus Walkway Camera' feed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VMS (Video Management System) application is open, and the camera feed labeled 'Campus Walkway Camera' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate the video timeline to locate the suspicious activities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The video timeline playhead is positioned somewhere between 29s and 141s, showing frames of pedestrians on a campus walkway.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a bookmark on the video timeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bookmark creation dialog or a new bookmark indicator is visible on the VMS timeline.

task_completion.description: Name the bookmark 'Suspicious Activity' and cover the correct time window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bookmark dialog shows the name 'Suspicious Activity' and the start/end times encompass the anomaly window (between ~29s and ~141s).

task_completion.description: Open a text editor to write the incident report at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) or terminal is open, showing the file path '/home/ga/suspicious_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write a detailed report including timings and descriptions of the abnormal actions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor contains a written report that explicitly mentions timings and describes specific abnormal behaviors such as throwing bags, running in the wrong direction, throwing objects, or loitering.

integrity.description: The agent used the VMS graphical interface to view the camera and create the bookmark.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the VMS GUI being interacted with (clicking timeline, opening dialogs) rather than just terminal commands executing API calls to create bookmarks.

integrity.description: The agent actually viewed the video footage to identify the anomalies.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actual video frames of the campus walkway are visible in the VMS viewer, proving the agent analyzed the visual content rather than guessing based on metadata.

integrity.description: The agent manually created and wrote the incident report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or standard file writing command is visible on screen with the typed report, indicating it was not generated by an automated script bypassing the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target camera is 'Campus Walkway Camera'. The video contains 4 distinct anomaly intervals (throwing bags, running wrong direction, throwing objects, loitering) occurring between roughly 29s and 141s into the 159.7s video. The bookmark must be named 'Suspicious Activity'. The incident report must be saved to '/home/ga/suspicious_report.txt' and describe these specific actions and their timings.
